summary: A single-story bunker-style structure with a rectangular footprint, featuring a sunken foundation with gravel perimeter, stone brick walls, and a flat roof with dark wood trim. The building has five evenly-spaced window openings on the visible long side and appears to be a fortified or underground facility.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box, partially sunken into terrain

elements: five rectangular windows with glass panes
flat roof with dark wood border trim
possible entrance on side (view 4 shows door-like opening)
rebuild_hints: Excavate rectangular pit approximately 14×8 blocks, depth 3-4 blocks below surface
Line foundation with gravel blocks around perimeter
Build stone brick walls 3 blocks high from foundation floor
Space five window openings evenly across long wall (approximately 2 blocks wide, 2 blocks tall each)
Install glass panes in window frames
Cap roof with flat structure using stone blocks
Add dark wood (dark oak or nether wood) trim around roof edges
Ensure symmetrical window placement with consistent spacing
Consider adding door entrance on short or long side
Surround exterior with gravel or stone for finished appearance
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and floor plan not visible
Precise window dimensions and frame material (appears to be stone or wood frame)
Door location and type - view 4 suggests possible entrance but unclear
Whether structure extends below visible foundation level
Exact roof material composition (appears to be dark wood with stone border)
Interior ceiling height and any internal supports
Whether windows are simple openings or have shutters/covers
